cv_text:
## Nate Dowdall

<CANDIDATE_NAME>, ON | <EMAIL> | <PHONE> | LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/nate-dowdall/

## SUMMARY

Ph.D. candidate with 5+ years of experience designing micro/nanoparticles for drug delivery and immunomodulation. Skilled in experimental design, material characterization, and analytical problem solving. Strong communicator with a proven track record in technical writing, cross-disciplinary research, and academic -industry collaboration. Demonstrated leadership in mentoring junior scientists and managing research projects from conception to completion.

## EDUCATION

## McMaster University

<CANDIDATE_NAME>, ON

Ph.D., Chemical Engineering

September 2019 - Present

Dissertation: Beta-Glucan Particles for Cancer and Fibrosis Therapy

Supervisor: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>

GPA: 4.0/4.0

## Queen's University

Kingston, ON

B.A.Sc., Chemical Engineering 2018- 2019 Dean's Scholar List Supervisor: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>

September 2015 - April 2019

## SKILLS

Laboratory: Micro/nanoparticle formulation, particle sizing/imaging, aerosol engineering, polymer/polysaccharide chemistry, spheroid and ex vivo tissue models

Software: Microsoft Office, GraphPad, ImageJ, ChemDraw

## RESEARCH EXPERIENCE

Graduate Researcher

- Initiated and managed novel projects while mentoring undergraduate researchers, fostering their development through hands-on guidance and critical feedback
- Communicated complex data effectively across academic and industry teams, and effectively showcased major findings at multiple international conferences
- Developed expertise in nanoparticle design and formulation, including polyelectrolyte complexation, hydrophobic self-assembly, and flash nanoprecipitation

Kingston, ON

- Assisted professors and senior researchers in developing new protocols for extracting and analyzing quartz fluid inclusions
- Developed careful sample preparation techniques working in a Class 100 clean lab

- Built effective communication skills by providing technical knowledge to 100+ students in their first exposure to polymer science and engineering

## PUBLICATIONS

pitch_text:
## Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch)

Selected Job Offer Position: Scientist, Formulation Development (Target: Biotech/Pharma companies working with <CANDIDATE_NAME>) Key Requirements:

- PhD in Chemical Engineering, Pharmaceutical Sciences, or related.
- Experience with nanoparticle formulation and characterization (DLS, Imaging).
- Strong technical writing and ability to collaborate across teams.

## The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I am a Chemical Engineer who specializes in advanced particle design for drug delivery.

In my PhD at <CANDIDATE_NAME>, I focused on engineering beta-glucan micro- and nanoparticles to treat complex diseases like cancer and fibrosis. I didn't just mix chemicals; I mastered advanced processing techniques like aerosol engineering and Pressurized Gas eXpanded (PGX) liquid technology to create inhalable therapies. I know how to control particle size, morphology, and release profiles to get the biology to respond exactly how we want it to.

+4

Beyond the bench, I've spent the last year mentoring under the CEO of a biotech firm, ChitoLytic , where I learned to look at materials through a regulatory and commercial lens-understanding not just if we can make it, but how we get it FDA-approved.

I'm looking for an R&D role where I can apply this dual mindset-rigorous formulation science and strategic application-to help your team develop the next generation of therapeutics."

<LOCATION>

clarify_text.ikigai:
My Ikigai is engineering precise material solutions for complex biological problems .

The central theme of my career is "Formulation and Communication." My passion lies in the minute details of nanoparticle design -taking a polymer like beta-glucan and engineering it to deliver a drug exactly where it needs to go. My talent is two-fold: the technical rigor to conduct cross-disciplinary research (from aerosol engineering to immunomodulation) , and the communication skills to translate those findings for diverse audiences, from undergraduate students to industry CEOs.

+4

The world needs novel drug delivery systems to treat conditions like cancer and fibrosis. By combining my expertise in polymer chemistry with my growing interest in scientific consulting , I can create a lasting impact: developing therapies that are not only scientifically novel but regulatory-compliant and market-ready. To align my career with this, I am ready to transition from the "exploration" phase of my PhD to the "application" phase of Industry R&D, leveraging my recent mentorship experience to navigate the biotech landscape.

+3
clarify_text.ideal_day:
My ideal day is a blend of technical innovation and strategic collaboration .

It starts in the lab, working on a microparticle formulation. I am setting up a Flash Nanoprecipitation run to create a new batch of drug-loaded carriers, analyzing the particle size distribution to ensure uniformity.

+2

By mid-day, I shift to data analysis using GraphPad , interpreting the results of a recent ex vivo tissue model experiment. In the afternoon, I transition to a "Consultant" mindset-similar to my work with ChitoLytic-meeting with a cross-functional team to discuss the regulatory hurdles (FDA) for a new biomaterial. The day ends by reviewing a junior colleague's technical report, providing the same critical feedback I used to give my students.

+3
clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career Activation): Transition from "passive looking" to "active applying" by submitting 5 high-quality applications to biotech companies dealing with nanomaterials/polymers by May 1, 2026.

+1

- Measurable: 5 applications submitted.
- Attainable: I have been watching the market for a year; I know what is out there.
- Goal 2 (Academic Completion): Finalize my PhD dissertation on "Beta-Glucan Particles for Cancer and Fibrosis Therapy" and defend by December 2026.
- Measurable: Thesis submitted and defense scheduled.
- Realistic: I have already published major parts of this work.

+1

- Goal 3 (Industry Readiness): Conduct two "informational interviews" with connections from my ChitoLytic network to better understand the specific hiring requirements for R&D Managers in Pharma by June 2026.

+1

- Measurable: Two meetings held.
- Time-bound: By June 2026.

preferences.target_companies: Not any specific companies, but I am generally interested in biotech companies, pharma companies, as well as companies that deal with nanomaterials and/or polymer materials (natural or synthetic) that aren't necessarily in the biotech space.
